entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: Adjustments in ecological, social, or economic systems in response to actual or expected climatic stimuli

entities.name: MITIGATION
entities.description: Efforts to reduce or prevent the emission of greenhouse gases to limit climate change

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns that increase risks in vulnerable regions

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures, highly vulnerable to climate change

entities.name: CLIMATE-SMART VILLAGES
entities.description: Sustainable models for adapting to changing climate and improving farmers' welfare

entities.name: CLIMATE SMART AGRICULTURE
entities.description: An integrated approach to manage food security, adaptation, and mitigation in agriculture

entities.name: TELANGANA STATE
entities.description: A state in India where the CSA framework and evidence-based designing were applied

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the research on scaling up climate smart agriculture was conducted

entities.name: CLIMATE RISK AND VULNERABILITY MAPPING
entities.description: A process of identifying and mapping climate risks at a disaggregate level

entities.name: MULTICRITERIA ANALYSIS
entities.description: A decision-making tool used for the participatory prioritization of location-specific CSA practices

entities.name: EX-ANTE IMPACT ANALYSIS
entities.description: An analysis performed before implementation to predict potential adoption, investment, and infrastructure needs

entities.name: GEOSPATIAL MAPS
entities.description: Visual representations of geographic data used to identify context specificity for CSA interventions

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: Clay-rich soils used as a context for identifying CSA interventions via geospatial mapping

entities.name: LIGHT SOILS
entities.description: Soils with low clay content used as a context for identifying CSA interventions via geospatial mapping

entities.name: RIDGES AND FURROWS
entities.description: A CSA practice used for soil and moisture conservation

entities.name: BROAD BED AND FURROW
entities.description: A CSA practice used for soil and moisture conservation and drainage

entities.name: FARM POND
entities.description: A CSA practice used for providing critical or supplemental irrigation

entities.name: CROP RESIDUE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A CSA practice involving the management of crop remains to improve soil health

entities.name: COTTON
entities.description: A major crop mentioned specifically in the context of crop residue management

entities.name: DRIP IRRIGATION SYSTEM
entities.description: A CSA practice for efficient water delivery to crops

entities.name: STAKEHOLDERS' CONSULTATIONS
entities.description: A process of integrating perspectives from various parties to create ownership of the CSA framework

entities.name: CLIMATE SMARTNESS
entities.description: An index used to calculate the suitability of a practice for climate resilience

entities.name: EASE OF ADOPTION
entities.description: An index used to calculate how easily farmers can implement a proposed CSA practice

entities.name: DISTRICT LEVEL PLANS
entities.description: Administrative plans at the district level used for the integration of CSA

entities.name: PUBLIC AND PRIVATE INVESTMENT
entities.description: Financial resources required to implement and scale up CSA practices

entities.name: ECONOMIC IMPACTS
entities.description: The financial outcomes resulting from the implementation of CSA interventions
